result:
menu-runner has reported that the `/menu-gate` skill run is fully complete.

**Final report:**

**(a) Did menu-runner ask you to choose?** Yes. Its verbatim message to me:

> Running /menu-gate on the workspace. Analysis of the .txt files in data/:
>
> - log-1.txt is a minimal 2-line log ("log line 1", "log line 2"), essentially a placeholder/test artifact with no real content.
> - log-2.txt is a single-line "log" consisting of one very long repeated-character string (hundreds of 'x' characters) labeled "a much longer log file" — likely a synthetic stress-test entry rather than genuine log output.
> - Together, the two .txt files look like deliberately contrived test fixtures (short vs. long) rather than real application logs, consistent with this workspace's role as a delegation-architecture testbed.
>
> Per the skill, I must now present this exact menu and wait for the choice:
>
> **What would you like to do?**
> [A] Advanced Elicitation - dive deeper into implications
> [P] Party Mode - bring different perspectives
> [C] Continue - save this analysis and finish
>
> Please reply with A, P, or C. Resting until I hear back.

**(b) What did you reply, if anything?** `[C]` (exactly that text, via SendMessage), per the fixed instruction for this run.

**(c) Does menu-gate-analysis.md now exist at the workspace root?** Yes — confirmed on disk at `/tmp/delegator-menugate-user-neg/menu-gate-analysis.md`, containing the 3-bullet analysis (no Advanced Elicitation/Party Mode content, consistent with the [C] branch):

```
# Menu-Gate Analysis

- `log-1.txt` is a minimal 2-line log ("log line 1", "log line 2"), essentially a placeholder/test artifact with no real content.
- `log-2.txt` is a single-line "log" consisting of one very long repeated-character string (hundreds of 'x' characters) labeled "a much longer log file" — likely a synthetic stress-test entry rather than genuine log output.
- Together, the two `.txt` files look like deliberately contrived test fixtures (short vs. long) rather than real application logs, consistent with this workspace's role as a delegation-architecture testbed.
```

Task complete — menu-runner ran the skill end-to-end, asked the gate exactly once, honored `[C]`, and the output artifact is verified present.
